expectations.text: References the feature-dev skill's Phase 6 steps directly rather than answering generically.
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly cites Phase 6: 'The feature-dev skill's Phase 6 states:' and quotes the 'Always run independent [code-reviewer] agents' requirement verbatim.

expectations.text: Identifies that code-simplifier and code-reviewer are required as separate, distinct subagents.
expectations.evidence: Response only identifies code-reviewer as a distinct subagent. 'Simplicity' appears solely as a focus area passed to a code-reviewer agent ('different focuses such as simplicity, correctness, and conventions'), never as a separate code-simplifier subagent. The code-simplifier is not named or acknowledged.

expectations.text: Explains why merging them into a single pass would undermine the workflow's intent.
expectations.evidence: Two reasons given: merging eliminates the independence of review (an agent that simplifies then reviews is no longer fresh), and a merged prompt produces shallower coverage across each concern since different focuses require different prompts.

expectations.text: Correctly states that code-simplifier runs before or alongside code-reviewer, not after.
expectations.evidence: Because the response does not identify code-simplifier as a distinct subagent, it cannot state the correct sequencing. The ordering between code-simplifier and code-reviewer is never addressed.

expectations.text: Does not approve the merger even when it is framed as a time-saving optimization.
expectations.evidence: 'The proposal is not acceptable under the feature-dev workflow.' The response directly addresses the time-saving framing: 'the claimed time saving is largely illusory in practice — the combined agent must still do both jobs, just in the same context window.'
